gotchas: **Gotcha — macOS has no `timeout`/`gtimeout`.** Don't wrap python runs in `timeout`; the script's own per-call `asyncio.wait_for` is the right cap. Also `rg -rn` is blocked (the `-r` = replace guard) — split grep from any heredoc.

**Resumable judge cache paid off conceptually** — keying each verdict by judge+row+labels means a 429/abort mid-run continues from cache rather than re-paying ~700 calls (the subagent-fleet 429 lesson applied to direct API fan-out; capped concurrency at 6/judge).

**The error profile was crisp because the sample was broad.** Auditing worst-sim AND mid-sim deviations (not just the cherry-picked worst) is what let the "no genuine model errors, it's all input quality" claim survive the full adjudication, and surfaced the tight Brakes/Baer signature.

**Scratchpad is ephemeral.** Analysis scripts (`score_validation.py`, `adjudicate.py`, `build_report_v2.py`) and the per-row verdicts (`adjudication_results.json`, `verdicts_cache.json`) live in this session's scratchpad and will be cleaned. The durable artifact is the report in `~/Downloads`. If the adjudication must be reproduced or extended (e.g. tighten the EXACT misfit CI beyond n=60, or human-review the 6 errors), the judge will need to be rebuilt — the verdict cache is resumable only while the scratchpad survives.
